methodsAttempted.notes: Fetched robots.txt. Public content was allowed except disallowed /search/ paths, which were not crawled.

methodsAttempted.query: site:westminster.ac.uk ("generative AI" OR "ChatGPT" OR "artificial intelligence") (student OR assessment OR policy OR guidance) "University of Westminster"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official student GenAI guidance page, official student PDF, official Library GenAI guide, and duplicate blog/news pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:westminster.ac.uk "Policy in Relation to the Safe Use of Generative AI" "University of Westminster"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the formal GenAI policy PDF, research guideline DOCX, doctoral researcher PDF, and IT acceptable-use references.

methodsAttempted.query: site:westminster.ac.uk "Generative AI" "staff" "University of Westminster" OR site:westminster.ac.uk "AI" "teaching" "University of Westminster"
methodsAttempted.notes: Search primarily returned blog category and post pages; no stronger staff-specific formal teaching policy page was found than the formal GenAI policy and research guidance.

methodsAttempted.query: links from official student GenAI guidance page
methodsAttempted.notes: Official student page linked the student GenAI PDF and doctoral researcher GenAI PDF.

artifacts.summary: Official Westminster sources provide a formal GenAI safe-use policy, student GenAI assessment guidance, doctoral researcher guidance, academic research guidelines, and a library ethics/rules guide. Blog and news variants were recorded as lower-specificity or duplicate sources and not used for claims.

artifacts.claimText: University of Westminster's GenAI policy applies to students, colleagues, third-party suppliers, and partners, and frames GenAI as an assistant or creative partner rather than a replacement for human ingenuity.

citation.citationTitle: Policy in relation to the safe use of Generative AI (GenAI)

artifacts.claimText: University of Westminster's GenAI policy requires GenAI-related data to be handled in line with data protection and security requirements, and says high-risk GenAI systems should not go into production without remediation.

citation.citationTitle: Policy in relation to the safe use of Generative AI (GenAI)

artifacts.claimText: Westminster student guidance says students must disclose any use of GenAI for assessed coursework and remain responsible for the originality, accuracy, and integrity of their work.

citation.citationTitle: Student Guidance: Using Artificial Intelligence Tools in Your Studies

artifacts.claimText: Westminster's student GenAI rules identify using AI to write essays, reports, or code on a student's behalf, using AI to carry out analysis or arguments, and submitting AI-generated content without declaring it as not permitted.

citation.citationTitle: GenAI: Ethics and Rules - Generative AI - LibGuides at University of Westminster

artifacts.claimText: Westminster's academic research GenAI guidance tells researchers and academic staff not to enter confidential or sensitive data into public or commercial generative AI tools, and to have specialist tools assessed by ISS when confidential data is involved.

citation.citationTitle: Resources and Guidelines for Using Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Academic Research

artifacts.claimText: Westminster doctoral researcher guidance says doctoral assessments and research outputs must be the researcher's own original work, and that using GenAI to generate doctoral research content without agreement and disclosure is academic misconduct.

citation.citationTitle: Guidance for Doctoral Researchers on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy in relation to the safe use of Generative AI (GenAI)
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It applies to all students. colleagues, third-party suppliers, and partners engaged in using or developing such systems within or on behalf of our institution.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The policy applies to students, colleagues, suppliers, and partners using or developing GenAI for or within Westminster.

citation.citationTitle: Policy in relation to the safe use of Generative AI (GenAI)

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy in relation to the safe use of Generative AI (GenAI)
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: All data used in the development and operation of GenAI systems must respect the privacy and rights of individuals involved and be securely transmitted, stored and managed in accordance with the University of Westminster's Data Protection Policy and relevant data protection laws.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The policy requires GenAI data handling to follow privacy, security, and data protection requirements.

citation.citationTitle: Policy in relation to the safe use of Generative AI (GenAI)

artifacts.sourceTitle: Student Guidance: Using Artificial Intelligence Tools in Your Studies
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: With every assessed submission where AI was used, you must include: Artificial Intelligence Use Declaration ... I confirm that I have not used AI to generate content that I am presenting as my own work.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students must include an AI-use declaration for assessed submissions where AI was used.

citation.citationTitle: Student Guidance: Using Artificial Intelligence Tools in Your Studies

artifacts.sourceTitle: GenAI: Ethics and Rules - Generative AI - LibGuides at University of Westminster
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: At the University of Westminster, the following is NOT permitted: Using AI to write essays, reports, or code on your behalf ... Using AI to carry out your analysis or construct arguments ... Submitting AI-generated content without declaring it.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The library guide lists specific student GenAI uses that are not permitted.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Rules at the University of Westminster Concerning the Use of GenAI by Students

citation.citationTitle: GenAI: Ethics and Rules - Generative AI - LibGuides at University of Westminster

artifacts.sourceTitle: Resources and Guidelines for Using Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Academic Research
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Do not enter confidential or sensitive data, including non-public research data, personal information, or any other data classified as confidential, into public or commercial generative AI tools.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Research guidance tells researchers not to enter confidential or sensitive data into public or commercial AI tools.

citation.citationTitle: Resources and Guidelines for Using Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Academic Research

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for Doctoral Researchers on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: In summary Using GenAI to generate content for your doctoral research without agreement and disclosure is unethical and violates academic and research integrity. Doing this is academic misconduct.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Doctoral guidance says GenAI-generated doctoral research content without agreement and disclosure is academic misconduct.

citation.citationTitle: Guidance for Doctoral Researchers on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence

artifacts.notes: Claim is limited to the formal policy scope and framing language.
Claim combines direct policy text on data protection/security and high-risk remediation without overstating implementation status.
Student disclosure requirement is directly stated in official 2025-26 student guidance.
Claim is scoped to the listed prohibited student uses on the official library rules page.
Research claim is limited to official academic research guidance and does not imply a broader tool approval list.
Doctoral claim is limited to official Graduate School guidance for doctoral researchers.

artifacts.title: University of Westminster GenAI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Draft staged evidence for University of Westminster identifies a formal safe-use GenAI policy, student assessment disclosure and misconduct guidance, research data safeguards, and doctoral researcher rules from official university sources.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Blog/news/category pages were not used as claim evidence where more direct official policy and guidance sources were available.
Blackboard and other login-gated learning resources linked from official pages were not accessed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
